task_title: Audit regression test files (6 files, ~1889 lines)

findings.title: Inverted assertion: regression test passes when fix is broken
findings.problem: test_lifespan_allows_localhost_for_ollama mocks validate_url then asserts mock_validate.assert_not_called(). This means the test PASSES when validate_url is NOT called (fix broken) and would only fail if the mock is called (fix working). The regression test has the assertion backwards — it guards against the fix being applied, not against the fix being reverted.
findings.fix: Change to mock_validate.assert_called() with verification that allow_local=True was passed. Alternatively, remove the mock and test the actual runtime behavior.
findings.evidence: mock_validate.assert_not_called()  # Wrong: passes when fix is reverted

findings.title: Mock bypasses actual settings migration code
findings.problem: test_settings_migration_from_model_path_to_gguf_path mocks _load_settings to return pre-migrated data, then checks that the code handles the mocked return value. This bypasses the actual migration logic. If the migration code were reverted, this test would still pass because it never exercises the real _load_settings.
findings.fix: Remove the mock and test actual migration by loading a settings file with old format, verifying the migration produces the new format.

findings.title: Test creates expected dict but never calls actual code
findings.problem: test_app_controller_initializes_with_correct_path_parameter creates an expected dict and asserts it equals itself. It never invokes any actual code — this is a tautology that always passes regardless of the fix state.
findings.fix: Rewrite to actually call _initialize_engine and verify the RAGEngine constructor arguments.

findings.title: Lifespan async context not executed — tests source strings instead
findings.problem: Multiple tests in test_defect_002 use inspect.getsource() to check if 'RAG_GGUF_PATH' appears in the source code, rather than running the lifespan context manager and verifying runtime behavior. The comment 'We can't easily run async here without pytest-asyncio' documents that the test cannot verify the fix at runtime.
findings.fix: Install pytest-asyncio and run the lifespan context with mocked env vars to verify RAGEngine receives gguf_path from RAG_GGUF_PATH env var.
findings.ai_pattern: Static analysis instead of runtime test

findings.title: String inspection verifies source text, not runtime behavior
findings.problem: test_rag_engine_initialized_with_gguf_from_env uses inspect.getsource() to check for 'RAG_GGUF_PATH' in api_server.py source. This detects if the variable name exists in source but doesn't verify it's actually read, parsed, or passed to RAGEngine.
findings.fix: Replace with runtime test using mocked environment.
findings.ai_pattern: Static analysis instead of runtime test

findings.title: Env var completeness test uses source string matching
findings.problem: test_api_server_environment_completeness checks if 12 RAG_* variable names appear in source via inspect.getsource(). This detects comments and string literals containing the variable names, not actual usage. Variables could be documented but not wired.
findings.fix: Replace with runtime tests that set each env var and verify it affects engine behavior.

findings.title: Tests document feature gaps rather than catching regressions
findings.problem: test_gui_and_api_upload_capabilities_aligned and test_upload_feature_parity_matrix explicitly verify that capability mismatches EXIST ('Expected mismatch found'). These tests PASS whether or not any fix is applied — they're feature documentation, not regression guards.
findings.fix: Move to a separate 'feature_gaps' or 'known_limitations' test category. Write actual regression tests that verify specific behaviors don't regress.
findings.ai_pattern: Testing for bug presence instead of absence

findings.title: Test accepts non-existent entry point ui/app.py
findings.problem: test_afomis_spec_entry_point_documented accepts 'ui/app.py' as a valid entry point, but this file doesn't exist in the repository. The test would pass with an invalid reference.
findings.fix: Remove 'ui/app.py' from acceptable values. The actual entry point is 'main.py'.

findings.title: Documentation test checks docstring presence, not functionality
findings.problem: test_validate_url_documentation only checks that 'allow_local' appears in the function docstring. Docstring presence doesn't verify the parameter works.
findings.fix: Merge with test_validate_url_signature_accepts_allow_local which already validates the parameter exists.

findings.title: IP classification test tests stdlib, not application code
findings.problem: test_private_ip_detection_accuracy tests the ipaddress module directly, not how validate_url uses it. Removing the ipaddress usage from validate_url would not cause this test to fail.
findings.fix: Rename to clarify scope, or add integration test verifying validate_url correctly delegates to ipaddress.
